for_verification_agent.acceptance_criteria: A single compose command brings up all four services (engine, api, agents, ui) networked together, and the UI can successfully reach the api/ gateway.
for_verification_agent.test_spec: docker compose up followed by a scripted smoke check (curl api/ health route, load ui/ root page) confirms all services are reachable.

how_to_reproduce.manual: cd deploy && docker compose up -d --build ; docker compose ps  (expect all 4 services 'healthy')
how_to_reproduce.scripted: ./deploy/smoke-test.sh  (brings the stack up, polls health, curls api /health + ui /, tears down; prints 'SMOKE TEST PASSED' on success)

ports.ui: host 8081 -> container 80 (nginx), root page + reverse-proxied /health and /query

key_design_decisions_to_check: deploy/nginx.conf: /query path is proxied to api unconditionally; api's real 405 (GET/non-POST) response is intercepted via `error_page 405 = @spa_shell` and internally rewritten to index.html so react-router's client-side /query view still renders on direct navigation/hard-refresh. Verify this by: curl -X GET http://localhost:8081/query (expect 200, SPA html) vs curl -X POST http://localhost:8081/query (expect proxied to api, non-2xx acceptable if no Ollama daemon is running -- see caveat below).

deploy/nginx.conf: /graph, /files, /admin deliberately have NO dedicated proxy location (fall through to the SPA catch-all `location / { try_files $uri /index.html; }`) because api/routes/query.go's RegisterRoutes does not implement handlers for those paths yet -- confirmed out of scope in requirement.md.

deploy/docker-compose.yml: api service gets QUERY_PIPELINE_ADDR=agents:50052 set (not present in api.Dockerfile itself) so api's /query route actually dials the agents service in this compose stack rather than agents being built/started/healthchecked but unused.

deploy/docker-compose.yml: agents service gets LLM_PROVIDER=${LLM_PROVIDER:-ollama} (default, no env_file directive -- uses plain ${VAR:-default} substitution so a missing deploy/.env does not error).
depends_on with condition: service_healthy is used at every edge (api/agents depend on engine; ui depends on api) -- Compose v2.34.0 feature, confirmed installed and working in this run.
for_verification_agent.known_caveat_not_a_defect: POST /query end-to-end (ui -> api -> agents) returns HTTP 500 in this sandbox because no local Ollama daemon is listening on localhost:11434 inside the agents container -- this is an environment/runtime-config gap, not a compose-wiring defect (LLM_PROVIDER=ollama is a wiring default per this subtask's instructions, not a promise of a working local Ollama daemon). The request DOES traverse the full ui->api->agents chain without any nginx/network-level failure (no 502/504, no connection-refused between containers), which is this subtask's actual acceptance bar ('the UI can successfully reach the api/ gateway'). Verification agent should confirm reachability (no proxy/network errors), not a successful LLM answer.

self_consistency_summary.all_four_services_healthy: engine, api, agents, ui all reached STATUS 'healthy' via docker compose ps
self_consistency_summary.smoke_test_script_actually_run: PASS -- deploy/smoke-test.sh executed for real, printed 'SMOKE TEST PASSED: engine, api, agents, ui all reachable', teardown via trap ran cleanly
self_consistency_summary.post_teardown_state: clean -- no leftover deploy-* containers or hivemind network after final `docker compose down -v`

not_in_scope_this_subtask: subtask 6.2.3 (real deploy target), separately blocked on an open question
Getting a real local Ollama daemon (or other real LLM provider) running so /query returns a genuine synthesized answer -- reachability only, per acceptance criteria
